skill.name: onboard
skill.description: Design or improve onboarding flows, empty states, and first-time user experiences. Helps users get started successfully and understand value quickly.
skill.summary: Design or improve onboarding flows, empty states, and first-time user experiences. Helps users get started successfully and understand value quickly.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 22 potential security issues, all confirmed as false positives after manual review. The skill file contains only Markdown documentation with code examples demonstrating UX patterns. No executable code or security risks present.

content.user_title: Design User Onboarding Flows That Drive Adoption
content.value_statement: Users abandon products when they cannot understand value quickly. This skill helps you create onboarding experiences that guide users to their first success without overwhelming them.
actual_capabilities: Design comprehensive onboarding flows from welcome screen to first success
Create effective empty states that guide users to take action
Plan guided tours and walkthroughs for complex interfaces
Apply progressive disclosure to avoid overwhelming users
Design interactive tutorials for hands-on learning
Implement contextual tooltips and feature discovery patterns
limitations: Cannot implement actual frontend code or components
Does not provide user testing or analytics data
Cannot customize for specific brand guidelines without context
Requires integration with existing design system for implementation

use_cases.title: Launch a new SaaS product feature
use_cases.description: Product managers and designers need to onboard users to a new feature effectively, ensuring adoption without overwhelming existing users.

use_cases.title: Improve empty state conversion rates
use_cases.description: UX designers want to transform blank screens into opportunities that guide users to create their first project or document.

use_cases.title: Reduce user drop-off during signup
use_cases.description: Startup founders need to optimize the initial user experience to increase activation rates and reduce time-to-value.

prompt_templates.title: Design First-Run Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an onboarding flow for a new user signing up for our product. Target users are beginners who have never used this type of tool before. Focus on getting them to their first success quickly.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user signs up and needs immediate guidance

prompt_templates.title: Create Empty State Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an empty state for when users first access the projects dashboard. Include what will appear there, why it matters, and clear next steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: User views blank dashboard for first time

prompt_templates.title: Plan Feature Tour
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a guided tour for our new collaboration feature. The interface is complex with multiple panels. Keep it under 7 steps and make it interactive.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Onboarding Drop-off
prompt_templates.prompt: Users are dropping off during onboarding at step 3 of 5. Analyze potential friction points and suggest improvements to reduce abandonment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Analytics show completion rate is too low

output_examples.input: Design an empty state for a project management dashboard
output: **Description**: Projects help you organize your work and collaborate with your team.
**Actions**: [Create your first project] or [Start from template]

output_examples.input: Create a 3-step onboarding flow for a photo editor app
output: - Value prop: Edit stunning photos in seconds
- Time estimate: 2 minutes to first edit
- Interactive crop tool demo with sample image
- Users actually crop, don't just watch
- Guide to apply filter and export
- Celebration: You're ready! Upload your own photo.

best_practices: Get users to their first success as quickly as possible - teach the minimum needed to deliver value
Make onboarding optional when possible - let experienced users skip and explore independently
Use progressive disclosure - teach features when users need them, not all at once
anti_patterns: Block all UI during tours - let users explore and interact while learning
Show the same tooltip repeatedly - track dismissals and respect user preferences
Separate tutorial mode from real product - let users learn with actual functionality

faq.question: How long should onboarding take?
faq.answer: Aim for users to reach their first success within 2-5 minutes. Longer onboarding creates drop-off. Focus on the 20% of features that deliver 80% of value.

faq.question: Should onboarding be required or optional?
faq.answer: Make it optional when possible. Let experienced users skip. For required steps, keep them minimal and explain why each question matters.

faq.question: How do I handle returning users?
faq.answer: Track completion in local storage. Never show initial onboarding twice. Returning users should see feature discovery, not basics.

faq.question: What's the difference between empty states and onboarding?
faq.answer: Empty states are onboarding opportunities that appear when a feature has no data. They should explain what goes there and provide a clear call-to-action.

faq.question: When should I use guided tours vs tooltips?
faq.answer: Use tours for complex interfaces or major changes. Use tooltips for individual features when users first encounter them. Always allow dismissal.

faq.question: How do I measure onboarding success?
faq.answer: Track completion rate, time to value, skip rate, and whether users take the desired next action. High skip rates may indicate onboarding is too long.
